output:
# solution.py
"""Team Collaboration Manager.

A thread-safe, in-memory collaboration platform that supports real-time
team coordination: user management, projects, tasks, threaded messaging with
attachments, a performance dashboard (completion metrics + peer ratings) and
exportable reports (CSV / PDF).

Design highlights
-----------------
* **Single shared re-entrant lock** -- every domain service
  (``users``, ``projects``, ``tasks``, ``messages``, ``performance``,
  ``reports``) shares the *same* :class:`threading.RLock` instance as the
  manager so that compound, cross-domain operations stay consistent under
  concurrent access.
* **Globally unique identifiers** -- a single monotonic counter (starting at
  ``1``) issues ids for every entity type, so ids never collide across users,
  projects, tasks or messages.
* **Defensive copies** -- every accessor returns a deep copy of stored state,
  so callers can mutate returned objects without corrupting the store.
* **Explicit, typed error hierarchy** rooted at
  :class:`TeamCollaborationError`.

The module depends only on the Python standard library.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import copy
import csv
import io
import threading
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Set

__all__ = [
    "TeamCollaborationManager",
    "TaskStatus",
    "UserRole",
    "ReportFormat",
    "ReportType",
    "TeamCollaborationError",
    "ValidationError",
    "NotFoundError",
    "AuthorizationError",
    "DuplicateError",
    "ConflictError",
    "Attachment",
    "User",
    "Project",
    "Task",
    "Message",
    "Rating",
]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Exceptions
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationError(Exception):
    """Base class for every error raised by this module."""


class ValidationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when input fails validation (bad value, type or invariant)."""


class NotFoundError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a referenced entity does not exist."""


class AuthorizationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an actor is not permitted to perform an operation."""


class DuplicateError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a uniqueness constraint would be violated."""


class ConflictError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an operation conflicts with current state."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enumerations
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class UserRole(Enum):
    """Role a user holds within the platform."""

    ADMIN = "admin"
    MANAGER = "manager"
    MEMBER = "member"
    VIEWER = "viewer"


class TaskStatus(Enum):
    """Lifecycle status of a task."""

    NOT_STARTED = "not_started"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    COMPLETED = "completed"


class ReportFormat(Enum):
    """Supported export formats for reports."""

    CSV = "csv"
    PDF = "pdf"


class ReportType(Enum):
    """Kinds of report the manager can produce."""

    PROJECT_PROGRESS = "project_progress"
    TEAM_PERFORMANCE = "team_performance"
    INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE = "individual_performance"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Data models
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@dataclass
class User:
    """A registered platform user."""

    id: int
    username: str
    email: str
    role: UserRole
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Project:
    """A project owned by a user; a container for tasks and messages."""

    id: int
    name: str
    description: str
    start_date: datetime
    end_date: datetime
    owner_id: int
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Task:
    """A unit of work that lives inside a project."""

    id: int
    project_id: int
    title: str
    description: str
    status: TaskStatus
    assignee_id: Optional[int]
    deadline: Optional[datetime]
    created_at: datetime
    started_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    completed_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Attachment:
    """File metadata attached to a message."""

    filename: str
    size: int
    uploaded_by: int
    uploaded_at: datetime
    content_type: str = "application/octet-stream"


@dataclass
class Message:
    """A message posted to either a project or a task."""

    id: int
    author_id: int
    body: str
    project_id: Optional[int]
    task_id: Optional[int]
    parent_id: Optional[int]
    created_at: datetime
    edited_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    attachments: List[Attachment] = field(default_factory=list)


@dataclass
class Rating:
    """A peer rating left by one user for another."""

    id: int
    rater_id: int
    ratee_id: int
    score: int
    feedback: str
    task_id: Optional[int]
    created_at: datetime


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Internal helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _Unset(object):
    """Sentinel used to distinguish "argument not supplied" from ``None``."""

    _instance = None

    def __new__(cls):
        if cls._instance is None:
            cls._instance = super(_Unset, cls).__new__(cls)
        return cls._instance

    def __repr__(self):  # pragma: no cover - cosmetic
        return "<UNSET>"


_UNSET = _Unset()


def _copy(obj):
    """Return an isolated deep copy of *obj*."""

    return copy.deepcopy(obj)


def _require_nonempty_str(value, field_name):
    """Validate that *value* is a non-blank string and return it stripped."""

    if not isinstance(value, str):
        raise ValidationError("{0} must be a string".format(field_name))
    stripped = value.strip()
    if not stripped:
        raise ValidationError("{0} must not be empty".format(field_name))
    return stripped


def _validate_email(email):
    """Validate an email address and return it normalised (stripped)."""

    if not isinstance(email, str):
        raise ValidationError("email must be a string")
    cleaned = email.strip()
    if not cleaned or "@" not in cleaned:
        raise ValidationError("invalid email address: {0!r}".format(email))
    return cleaned


def _validate_date_range(start, end):
    """Ensure *start* / *end* are datetimes and ``end >= start``."""

    if not isinstance(start, datetime) or not isinstance(end, datetime):
        raise ValidationError("start_date and end_date must be datetimes")
    if end < start:
        raise ValidationError("end_date must not be before start_date")


def _default_now():
    """Default clock used when the caller does not inject one."""

    return datetime.now()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Shared state
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _State:
    """Mutable store shared by every service behind a single lock.

    All index maintenance lives here so the various services cannot drift out
    of sync. Methods on this class assume the caller already holds ``lock``.
    """

    def __init__(self, now):
        self.lock = threading.RLock()
        self._now = now if callable(now) else _default_now
        self._id_counter = 0

        # Primary stores: id -> entity
        self.users: Dict[int, User] = {}
        self.projects: Dict[int, Project] = {}
        self.tasks: Dict[int, Task] = {}
        self.messages: Dict[int, Message] = {}
        self.ratings: Dict[int, Rating] = {}

        # Secondary indexes
        self.username_index: Dict[str, int] = {}
        self.email_index: Dict[str, int] = {}
        self.tasks_by_project: Dict[int, Set[int]] = {}
        self.tasks_by_assignee: Dict[int, Set[int]] = {}
        self.messages_by_project: Dict[int, Set[int]] = {}
        self.messages_by_task: Dict[int, Set[int]] = {}
        self.ratings_by_ratee: Dict[int, Set[int]] = {}

    # -- identity / time ----------------------------------------------------
    def now(self) -> datetime:
        return self._now()

    def next_id(self) -> int:
        self._id_counter += 1
        return self._id_counter

    # -- existence guards ---------------------------------------------------
    def require_user(self, user_id) -> User:
        user = self.users.get(user_id)
        if user is None:
            raise NotFoundError("user not found: {0}".format(user_id))
        return user

    def require_project(self, project_id) -> Project:
        project = self.projects.get(project_id)
        if project is None:
            raise NotFoundError("project not found: {0}".format(project_id))
        return project

    def require_task(self, task_id) -> Task:
        task = self.tasks.get(task_id)
        if task is None:
            raise NotFoundError("task not found: {0}".format(task_id))
        return task

    def require_message(self, message_id) -> Message:
        message = self.messages.get(message_id)
        if message is None:
            raise NotFoundError("message not found: {0}".format(message_id))
        return message

    # -- index maintenance --------------------------------------------------
    def index_task(self, task: Task) -> None:
        self.tasks_by_project.setdefault(task.project_id, set()).add(task.id)
        if task.assignee_id is not None:
            self.tasks_by_assignee.setdefault(task.assignee_id, set()).add(task.id)

    def set_task_assignee(self, task: Task, new_assignee: Optional[int]) -> None:
        if task.assignee_id == new_assignee:
            return
        if task.assignee_id is not None:
            current = self.tasks_by_assignee.get(task.assignee_id)
            if current is not None:
                current.discard(task.id)
        task.assignee_id = new_assignee
        if new_assignee is not None:
            self.tasks_by_assignee.setdefault(new_assignee, set()).add(task.id)

    def remove_task(self, task: Task) -> None:
        self.tasks.pop(task.id, None)
        in_project = self.tasks_by_project.get(task.project_id)
        if in_project is not None:
            in_project.discard(task.id)
        if task.assignee_id is not None:
            owned = self.tasks_by_assignee.get(task.assignee_id)
            if owned is not None:
                owned.discard(task.id)

    def remove_message(self, message: Message) -> None:
        self.messages.pop(message.id, None)
        if message.project_id is not None:
            in_project = self.messages_by_project.get(message.project_id)
            if in_project is not None:
                in_project.discard(message.id)
        if message.task_id is not None:
            in_task = self.messages_by_task.get(message.task_id)
            if in_task is not None:
                in_task.discard(message.id)

    # -- ordered retrieval --------------------------------------------------
    def sorted_entities(self, id_set: Set[int], store: Dict[int, object]) -> List[object]:
        return [store[i] for i in sorted(id_set) if i in store]


class _BaseService:
    """Common base wiring each service to the shared state and lock."""

    def __init__(self, state: _State):
        self._state = state
        self._lock = state.lock


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# User service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class UserService(_BaseService):
    """CRUD and lifecycle management for users."""

    def register(self, username, email, role=UserRole.MEMBER) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            name = _require_nonempty_str(username, "username")
            mail = _validate_email(email)
            if not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole instance")
            state = self._state
            if name in state.username_index:
                raise DuplicateError("username already in use: {0}".format(name))
            if mail in state.email_index:
                raise DuplicateError("email already in use: {0}".format(mail))
            user = User(
                id=state.next_id(),
                username=name,
                email=mail,
                role=role,
                created_at=state.now(),
            )
            state.users[user.id] = user
            state.username_index[name] = user.id
            state.email_index[mail] = user.id
            return _copy(user)

    def get(self, user_id) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            return _copy(self._state.require_user(user_id))

    def list_all(self) -> List[User]:
        with self._lock:
            state = self._state
            return [_copy(state.users[i]) for i in sorted(state.users)]

    def update(self, user_id, username=_UNSET, email=_UNSET, role=_UNSET) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            state = self._state
            user = state.require_user(user_id)

            new_username = user.username
            new_email = user.email
            new_role = user.role

            if username is not _UNSET:
                new_username = _require_nonempty_str(username, "username")
                holder = state.username_index.get(new_username)
                if holder is not None and holder != user_id:
                    raise DuplicateError(
                        "username already in use: {0}".format(new_username)
                    )
            if email is not _UNSET:
                new_email = _validate_email(email)
                holder = state.email_index.get(new_email)
                if holder is not None and holder != user_id:
                    raise DuplicateError(
                        "email already in use: {0}".format(new_email)
                    )
            if role is not _UNSET:
                if not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                    raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole instance")
                new_role = role

            if new_username != user.username:
                state.username_index.pop(user.username, None)
                state.username_index[new_username] = user_id
                user.username = new_username
            if new_email != user.email:
                state.email_index.pop(user.email, None)
                state.email_index[new_email] = user_id
                user.email = new_email
            user.role = new_role
            return _copy(user)

    def delete(self, user_id) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            state = self._state
            user = state.require_user(user_id)

            for project in state.projects.values():
                if project.owner_id == user_id:
                    raise ConflictError(
                        "user owns project {0}; reassign before deleting".format(
                            project.id
                        )
                    )

            for task_id in state.tasks_by_assignee.get(user_id, set()):
                task = state.tasks.get(task_id)
                if task is not None and task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    raise ConflictError(
                        "user has open task {0}; reassign before deleting".format(
                            task_id
                        )
                    )

            state.users.pop(user_id, None)
            state.username_index.pop(user.username, None)
            state.email_index.pop(user.email, None)
            return None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Project service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class ProjectService(_BaseService):
    """CRUD and cascade management for projects."""

    def create(self, name, start_date, end_date, owner_id, description="") -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            state = self._state
            project_name = _require_nonempty_str(name, "name")
            state.require_user(owner_id)
            _validate_date_range(start_date, end_date)
            project = Project(
                id=state.next_id(),
                name=project_name,
                description=description or "",
                start_date=start_date,
                end_date=end_date,
                owner_id=owner_id,
                created_at=state.now(),
            )
            state.projects[project.id] = project
            state.tasks_by_project.setdefault(project.id, set())
            state.messages_by_project.setdefault(project.id, set())
            return _copy(project)

    def get(self, project_id) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            return _copy(self._state.require_project(project_id))

    def list_all(self) -> List[Project]:
        with self._lock:
            state = self._state
            return [_copy(state.projects[i]) for i in sorted(state.projects)]

    def update(
        self,
        project_id,
        name=_UNSET,
        description=_UNSET,
        start_date=_UNSET,
        end_date=_UNSET,
    ) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            state = self._state
            project = state.require_project(project_id)

            new_name = project.name
            if name is not _UNSET:
                new_name = _require_nonempty_str(name, "name")

            new_start = project.start_date if start_date is _UNSET else start_date
            new_end = project.end_date if end_date is _UNSET else end_date
            _validate_date_range(new_start, new_end)

            if description is not _UNSET:
                project.description = description or ""
            project.name = new_name
            project.start_date = new_start
            project.end_date = new_end
            return _copy(project)

    def delete(self, project_id) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            state = self._state
            state.require_project(project_id)

            for task_id in list(state.tasks_by_project.get(project_id, set())):
                task = state.tasks.get(task_id)
                if task is None:
                    continue
                for message_id in list(state.messages_by_task.get(task_id, set())):
                    message = state.messages.get(message_id)
                    if message is not None:
                        state.remove_message(message)
                state.messages_by_task.pop(task_id, None)
                state.remove_task(task)
            state.tasks_by_project.pop(project_id, None)

            for message_id in list(state.messages_by_project.get(project_id, set())):
                message = state.messages.get(message_id)
                if message is not None:
                    state.remove_message(message)
            state.messages_by_project.pop(project_id, None)

            state.projects.pop(project_id, None)
            return None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Task service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TaskService(_BaseService):
    """Task creation, assignment, scheduling and status transitions."""

    def create(
        self, project_id, title, assignee_id=None, deadline=None, description=""
    ) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            state = self._state
            project = state.require_project(project_id)
            task_title = _require_nonempty_str(title, "title")
            if assignee_id is not None:
                state.require_user(assignee_id)
            if deadline is not None:
                if not isinstance(deadline, datetime):
                    raise ValidationError("deadline must be a datetime")
                if deadline < project.start_date:
                    raise ValidationError("deadline must not precede project start")
            task = Task(
                id=state.next_id(),
                project_id=project_id,
                title=task_title,
                description=description or "",
                status=TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED,
                assignee_id=assignee_id,
                deadline=deadline,
                created_at=state.now(),
                started_at=None,
                completed_at=None,
            )
            state.tasks[task.id] = task
            state.index_task(task)
            return _copy(task)

    def get(self, task_id) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            return _copy(self._state.require_task(task_id))

    def list_by_project(self, project_id) -> List[Task]:
        with self._lock:
            state = self._state
            state.require_project(project_id)
            ids = state.tasks_by_project.get(project_id, set())
            return [_copy(state.tasks[i]) for i in sorted(ids) if i in state.tasks]

    def list_by_assignee(self, assignee_id) -> List[Task]:
        with self._lock:
            state = self._state
            state.require_user(assignee_id)
            ids = state.tasks_by_assignee.get(assignee_id, set())
            return [_copy(state.tasks[i]) for i in sorted(ids) if i in state.tasks]

    def assign(self, task_id, assignee_id) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            state = self._state
            task = state.require_task(task_id)
            state.require_user(assignee_id)
            state.set_task_assignee(task, assignee_id)
            return _copy(task)

    def reassign(self, task_id, assignee_id) -> Task:
        # Reassignment shares the exact semantics of assignment: validate the
        # task and the new assignee, then move the index entry.
        return self.assign(task_id, assignee_id)

    def unassign(self, task_id) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            state = self._state
            task = state.require_task(task_id)
            state.set_task_assignee(task, None)
            return _copy(task)

    def set_deadline(self, task_id, deadline) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            state = self._state
            task = state.require_task(task_id)
            if deadline is not None:
                if not isinstance(deadline, datetime):
                    raise ValidationError("deadline must be a datetime")
                project = state.projects.get(task.project_id)
                if project is not None and deadline < project.start_date:
                    raise ValidationError("deadline must not precede project start")
            task.deadline = deadline
            return _copy(task)

    def update_status(self, task_id, status) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            state = self._state
            task = state.require_task(task_id)
            if not isinstance(status, TaskStatus):
                raise ValidationError("status must be a TaskStatus instance")
            now = state.now()
            if status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED:
                task.started_at = None
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = now
            task.status = status
            return _copy(task)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Message service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class MessageService(_BaseService):
    """Threaded messaging on projects and tasks, with attachments."""

    def _build_attachments(
        self, attachments, author_id, now
    ) -> List[Attachment]:
        built: List[Attachment] = []
        if not attachments:
            return built
        for item in attachments:
            try:
                filename, data = item
            except (TypeError, ValueError):
                raise ValidationError(
                    "each attachment must be a (filename, data) pair"
                )
            if not isinstance(filename, str) or not filename.strip():
                raise ValidationError("attachment filename must not be empty")
            size = len(data) if data is not None else 0
            built.append(
                Attachment(
                    filename=filename,
                    size=size,
                    uploaded_by=author_id,
                    uploaded_at=now,
                )
            )
        return built

    def post(
        self,
        author_id,
        body,
        project_id=None,
        task_id=None,
        parent_id=None,
        attachments=None,
    ) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            state = self._state
            state.require_user(author_id)

            if (project_id is None) == (task_id is None):
                raise ValidationError(
                    "exactly one of project_id or task_id must be provided"
                )
            if project_id is not None:
                state.require_project(project_id)
            if task_id is not None:
                state.require_task(task_id)

            now = state.now()
            built_attachments = self._build_attachments(attachments, author_id, now)
            body_text = body if isinstance(body, str) else ""
            if not body_text.strip() and not built_attachments:
                raise ValidationError(
                    "message must have a body or at least one attachment"
                )
            if parent_id is not None:
                state.require_message(parent_id)

            message = Message(
                id=state.next_id(),
                author_id=author_id,
                body=body_text,
                project_id=project_id,
                task_id=task_id,
                parent_id=parent_id,
                created_at=now,
                edited_at=None,
                attachments=built_attachments,
            )
            state.messages[message.id] = message
            if project_id is not None:
                state.messages_by_project.setdefault(project_id, set()).add(message.id)
            if task_id is not None:
                state.messages_by_task.setdefault(task_id, set()).add(message.id)
            return _copy(message)

    def edit(self, message_id, editor_id, new_body) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            state = self._state
            message = state.require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != editor_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may edit this message")
            if not isinstance(new_body, str) or not new_body.strip():
                raise ValidationError("message body must not be empty")
            message.body = new_body
            message.edited_at = state.now()
            return _copy(message)

    def add_attachment(self, message_id, user_id, filename, data) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            state = self._state
            message = state.require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != user_id:
                raise AuthorizationError(
                    "only the author may attach files to this message"
                )
            if not isinstance(filename, str) or not filename.strip():
                raise ValidationError("attachment filename must not be empty")
            size = len(data) if data is not None else 0
            message.attachments.append(
                Attachment(
                    filename=filename,
                    size=size,
                    uploaded_by=user_id,
                    uploaded_at=state.now(),
                )
            )
            return _copy(message)

    def list_by_project(self, project_id) -> List[Message]:
        with self._lock:
            state = self._state
            state.require_project(project_id)
            ids = state.messages_by_project.get(project_id, set())
            return [_copy(state.messages[i]) for i in sorted(ids) if i in state.messages]

    def list_by_task(self, task_id) -> List[Message]:
        with self._lock:
            state = self._state
            state.require_task(task_id)
            ids = state.messages_by_task.get(task_id, set())
            return [_copy(state.messages[i]) for i in sorted(ids) if i in state.messages]

    def delete(self, message_id, user_id) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            state = self._state
            message = state.require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != user_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may delete this message")
            state.remove_message(message)
            return None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Performance service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class PerformanceService(_BaseService):
    """Completion metrics, peer ratings and per-user dashboards."""

    def _assigned_tasks(self, user_id) -> List[Task]:
        state = self._state
        ids = state.tasks_by_assignee.get(user_id, set())
        return [state.tasks[i] for i in ids if i in state.tasks]

    def completion_rate(self, user_id) -> float:
        with self._lock:
            state = self._state
            state.require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._assigned_tasks(user_id)
            if not tasks:
                return 0.0
            completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
            return completed / len(tasks)

    def average_completion_time(self, user_id) -> Optional[float]:
        with self._lock:
            state = self._state
            state.require_user(user_id)
            durations = []
            for task in self._assigned_tasks(user_id):
                if (
                    task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                    and task.started_at is not None
                    and task.completed_at is not None
                ):
                    durations.append(
                        (task.completed_at - task.started_at).total_seconds()
                    )
            if not durations:
                return None
            return sum(durations) / len(durations)

    def submit_rating(
        self, rater_id, ratee_id, score, feedback="", task_id=None
    ) -> Rating:
        with self._lock:
            state = self._state
            state.require_user(rater_id)
            state.require_user(ratee_id)
            if rater_id == ratee_id:
                raise ConflictError("a user may not rate themselves")
            if isinstance(score, bool) or not isinstance(score, int):
                raise ValidationError("score must be an integer")
            if not 1 <= score <= 5:
                raise ValidationError("score must be between 1 and 5")
            if task_id is not None:
                state.require_task(task_id)
            rating = Rating(
                id=state.next_id(),
                rater_id=rater_id,
                ratee_id=ratee_id,
                score=score,
                feedback=feedback or "",
                task_id=task_id,
                created_at=state.now(),
            )
            state.ratings[rating.id] = rating
            state.ratings_by_ratee.setdefault(ratee_id, set()).add(rating.id)
            return _copy(rating)

    def _ratings_for(self, user_id) -> List[Rating]:
        state = self._state
        ids = state.ratings_by_ratee.get(user_id, set())
        return [state.ratings[i] for i in sorted(ids) if i in state.ratings]

    def ratings_summary(self, user_id) -> Dict[str, object]:
        with self._lock:
            state = self._state
            state.require_user(user_id)
            ratings = self._ratings_for(user_id)
            count = len(ratings)
            average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / count if count else 0.0
            )
            return {
                "count": count,
                "average": average,
                "ratings": [_copy(r) for r in ratings],
            }

    def user_dashboard(self, user_id) -> Dict[str, object]:
        with self._lock:
            state = self._state
            state.require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._assigned_tasks(user_id)
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
            in_progress = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS
            )
            not_started = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED
            )
            ratings = self._ratings_for(user_id)
            rating_count = len(ratings)
            rating_average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count
                if rating_count
                else 0.0
            )
            return {
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
                "not_started_tasks": not_started,
                "completion_rate": (completed / total) if total else 0.0,
                "average_completion_time": self.average_completion_time(user_id),
                "rating_count": rating_count,
                "rating_average": rating_average,
            }


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Report service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class ReportService(_BaseService):
    """Aggregated reporting plus CSV / PDF export."""

    def __init__(self, state: _State, performance: PerformanceService):
        super().__init__(state)
        self._performance = performance

    # -- builders -----------------------------------------------------------
    def project_progress(self, project_id) -> Dict[str, object]:
        with self._lock:
            state = self._state
            state.require_project(project_id)
            now = state.now()
            task_ids = state.tasks_by_project.get(project_id, set())
            tasks = [state.tasks[i] for i in sorted(task_ids) if i in state.tasks]

            status_counts = {status.value: 0 for status in TaskStatus}
            overdue = 0
            per_assignee: Dict[int, Dict[str, object]] = {}
            completed = 0
            for task in tasks:
                status_counts[task.status.value] += 1
                if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    completed += 1
                if (
                    task.deadline is not None
                    and task.deadline < now
                    and task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                ):
                    overdue += 1
                if task.assignee_id is not None:
                    bucket = per_assignee.setdefault(
                        task.assignee_id,
                        {"total": 0, "completed": 0, "completion_rate": 0.0},
                    )
                    bucket["total"] += 1
                    if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                        bucket["completed"] += 1
            for bucket in per_assignee.values():
                total = bucket["total"]
                bucket["completion_rate"] = (
                    bucket["completed"] / total if total else 0.0
                )

            total_tasks = len(tasks)
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "total_tasks": total_tasks,
                "status_counts": status_counts,
                "completion_rate": (completed / total_tasks) if total_tasks else 0.0,
                "overdue_tasks": overdue,
                "per_assignee": per_assignee,
            }

    def _row_for_user(self, user_id, task_subset) -> Dict[str, object]:
        state = self._state
        user = state.users.get(user_id)
        total = len(task_subset)
        completed = sum(
            1 for t in task_subset if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
        )
        durations = [
            (t.completed_at - t.started_at).total_seconds()
            for t in task_subset
            if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            and t.started_at is not None
            and t.completed_at is not None
        ]
        avg_time = sum(durations) / len(durations) if durations else None
        ratings = [
            state.ratings[i]
            for i in state.ratings_by_ratee.get(user_id, set())
            if i in state.ratings
        ]
        rating_count = len(ratings)
        rating_average = (
            sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count if rating_count else 0.0
        )
        return {
            "user_id": user_id,
            "username": user.username if user is not None else None,
            "total_tasks": total,
            "completed_tasks": completed,
            "completion_rate": (completed / total) if total else 0.0,
            "average_completion_time": avg_time,
            "rating_count": rating_count,
            "rating_average": rating_average,
        }

    def team_performance(self, project_id=None) -> Dict[str, object]:
        with self._lock:
            state = self._state
            if project_id is not None:
                state.require_project(project_id)
                task_ids = state.tasks_by_project.get(project_id, set())
                scoped_tasks = [
                    state.tasks[i] for i in task_ids if i in state.tasks
                ]
            else:
                scoped_tasks = list(state.tasks.values())

            grouped: Dict[int, List[Task]] = {}
            for task in scoped_tasks:
                if task.assignee_id is None:
                    continue
                grouped.setdefault(task.assignee_id, []).append(task)

            rows = [
                self._row_for_user(user_id, grouped[user_id])
                for user_id in sorted(grouped)
            ]
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "rows": rows,
            }

    def individual_performance(self, user_id) -> Dict[str, object]:
        with self._lock:
            state = self._state
            state.require_user(user_id)
            task_ids = state.tasks_by_assignee.get(user_id, set())
            tasks = [state.tasks[i] for i in sorted(task_ids) if i in state.tasks]

            total = len(tasks)
            completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
            in_progress = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS
            )
            not_started = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED
            )
            row = self._row_for_user(user_id, tasks)
            task_rows = [
                {
                    "task_id": t.id,
                    "title": t.title,
                    "status": t.status.value,
                    "project_id": t.project_id,
                    "deadline": t.deadline,
                    "started_at": t.started_at,
                    "completed_at": t.completed_at,
                }
                for t in tasks
            ]
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "user_id": user_id,
                "username": row["username"],
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
                "not_started_tasks": not_started,
                "completion_rate": row["completion_rate"],
                "average_completion_time": row["average_completion_time"],
                "rating_count": row["rating_count"],
                "rating_average": row["rating_average"],
                "tasks": task_rows,
            }

    # -- export -------------------------------------------------------------
    def export(self, report, report_format):
        if not isinstance(report, dict):
            raise ValidationError("report must be a report dictionary")
        if not isinstance(report_format, ReportFormat):
            raise ValidationError("report_format must be a ReportFormat instance")
        rows = self._report_to_rows(report)
        if report_format == ReportFormat.CSV:
            return self._rows_to_csv(rows)
        return self._rows_to_pdf(rows, report)

    def _report_to_rows(self, report) -> List[List[str]]:
        """Flatten any report dict into a list of CSV-style rows."""

        report_type = report.get("report_type")
        if report_type == ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value:
            header = [
                "user_id",
                "username",
                "total_tasks",
                "completed_tasks",
                "completion_rate",
                "average_completion_time",
                "rating_count",
                "rating_average",
            ]
            rows = [header]
            for entry in report.get("rows", []):
                rows.append([self._fmt(entry.get(key)) for key in header])
            return rows

        if report_type == ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value:
            rows = [["metric", "value"]]
            rows.append(["project_id", self._fmt(report.get("project_id"))])
            rows.append(["total_tasks", self._fmt(report.get("total_tasks"))])
            rows.append(
                ["completion_rate", self._fmt(report.get("completion_rate"))]
            )
            rows.append(["overdue_tasks", self._fmt(report.get("overdue_tasks"))])
            for status_value, count in sorted(
                report.get("status_counts", {}).items()
            ):
                rows.append(["status:" + str(status_value), self._fmt(count)])
            for assignee_id, stats in sorted(
                report.get("per_assignee", {}).items()
            ):
                rows.append(
                    [
                        "assignee:" + str(assignee_id),
                        "total={0} completed={1} rate={2}".format(
                            stats.get("total"),
                            stats.get("completed"),
                            self._fmt(stats.get("completion_rate")),
                        ),
                    ]
                )
            return rows

        if report_type == ReportType.INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE.value:
            rows = [
                [
                    "user_id",
                    "username",
                    "total_tasks",
                    "completed_tasks",
                    "in_progress_tasks",
                    "not_started_tasks",
                    "completion_rate",
                    "average_completion_time",
                    "rating_count",
                    "rating_average",
                ]
            ]
            rows.append([self._fmt(report.get(key)) for key in rows[0]])
            rows.append([])
            rows.append(["task_id", "title", "status", "deadline", "completed_at"])
            for task in report.get("tasks", []):
                rows.append(
                    [
                        self._fmt(task.get("task_id")),
                        self._fmt(task.get("title")),
                        self._fmt(task.get("status")),
                        self._fmt(task.get("deadline")),
                        self._fmt(task.get("completed_at")),
                    ]
                )
            return rows

        # Generic fallback: one key/value row per top-level item.
        rows = [["key", "value"]]
        for key in sorted(report.keys(), key=str):
            rows.append([self._fmt(key), self._fmt(report.get(key))])
        return rows

    @staticmethod
    def _fmt(value) -> str:
        if value is None:
            return ""
        if isinstance(value, float):
            # Keep ints clean, floats compact.
            if value.is_integer():
                return str(int(value))
            return "{0:.4f}".format(value)
        return str(value)

    @staticmethod
    def _rows_to_csv(rows) -> str:
        buffer = io.StringIO()
        writer = csv.writer(buffer)
        for row in rows:
            writer.writerow(row)
        return buffer.getvalue()

    @staticmethod
    def _rows_to_pdf(rows, report) -> bytes:
        """Render rows into a minimal but structurally valid PDF document."""

        lines = []
        title = str(report.get("report_type", "report"))
        lines.append(title)
        for row in rows:
            lines.append(" | ".join(str(cell) for cell in row))

        def escape(text):
            return (
                text.replace("\\", r"\\")
                .replace("(", r"\(")
                .replace(")", r"\)")
            )

        content_parts = ["BT", "/F1 11 Tf", "50 760 Td", "13 TL"]
        for index, line in enumerate(lines):
            safe = escape(line[:120])
            if index == 0:
                content_parts.append("({0}) Tj".format(safe))
            else:
                content_parts.append("T* ({0}) Tj".format(safe))
        content_parts.append("ET")
        content_stream = "\n".join(content_parts).encode("latin-1", "replace")

        objects = []
        objects.append(b"<< /Type /Catalog /Pages 2 0 R >>")
        objects.append(b"<< /Type /Pages /Kids [3 0 R] /Count 1 >>")
        objects.append(
            b"<< /Type /Page /Parent 2 0 R /MediaBox [0 0 612 792] "
            b"/Resources << /Font << /F1 5 0 R >> >> /Contents 4 0 R >>"
        )
        objects.append(
            b"<< /Length "
            + str(len(content_stream)).encode("ascii")
            + b" >>\nstream\n"
            + content_stream
            + b"\nendstream"
        )
        objects.append(
            b"<< /Type /Font /Subtype /Type1 /BaseFont /Helvetica >>"
        )

        buffer = bytearray()
        buffer += b"%PDF-1.4\n"
        offsets = []
        for index, body in enumerate(objects, start=1):
            offsets.append(len(buffer))
            buffer += "{0} 0 obj\n".format(index).encode("ascii")
            buffer += body
            buffer += b"\nendobj\n"

        xref_position = len(buffer)
        count = len(objects) + 1
        buffer += "xref\n0 {0}\n".format(count).encode("ascii")
        buffer += b"0000000000 65535 f \n"
        for offset in offsets:
            buffer += "{0:010d} 00000 n \n".format(offset).encode("ascii")
        buffer += (
            "trailer\n<< /Size {0} /Root 1 0 R >>\nstartxref\n{1}\n%%EOF\n".format(
                count, xref_position
            ).encode("ascii")
        )
        return bytes(buffer)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Manager facade
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationManager:
    """Public facade coordinating every collaboration service.

    A single :class:`threading.RLock` (exposed as ``_lock`` and shared by every
    sub-service) guards all mutations, making the manager safe to use from
    multiple threads.

    Parameters
    ----------
    now:
        Optional zero-argument callable returning the "current" ``datetime``.
        Injecting a clock makes time-dependent behaviour deterministic in
        tests. Defaults to :func:`datetime.datetime.now`.
    """

    def __init__(self, now=None):
        self._state = _State(now)
        self._lock = self._state.lock

        # Domain services -- every one shares ``self._lock``.
        self.users = UserService(self._state)
        self.projects = ProjectService(self._state)
        self.tasks = TaskService(self._state)
        self.messages = MessageService(self._state)
        self.performance = PerformanceService(self._state)
        self.reports = ReportService(self._state, self.performance)

    # -- users --------------------------------------------------------------
    def register_user(self, username, email, role=UserRole.MEMBER) -> User:
        return self.users.register(username, email, role)

    def get_user(self, user_id) -> User:
        return self.users.get(user_id)

    def list_users(self) -> List[User]:
        return self.users.list_all()

    def update_user(self, user_id, username=_UNSET, email=_UNSET, role=_UNSET) -> User:
        return self.users.update(user_id, username=username, email=email, role=role)

    def delete_user(self, user_id) -> None:
        return self.users.delete(user_id)

    # -- projects -----------------------------------------------------------
    def create_project(
        self, name, start_date, end_date, owner_id, description=""
    ) -> Project:
        return self.projects.create(name, start_date, end_date, owner_id, description)

    def get_project(self, project_id) -> Project:
        return self.projects.get(project_id)

    def list_projects(self) -> List[Project]:
        return self.projects.list_all()

    def update_project(
        self,
        project_id,
        name=_UNSET,
        description=_UNSET,
        start_date=_UNSET,
        end_date=_UNSET,
    ) -> Project:
        return self.projects.update(
            project_id,
            name=name,
            description=description,
            start_date=start_date,
            end_date=end_date,
        )

    def delete_project(self, project_id) -> None:
        return self.projects.delete(project_id)

    # -- tasks --------------------------------------------------------------
    def create_task(
        self, project_id, title, assignee_id=None, deadline=None, description=""
    ) -> Task:
        return self.tasks.create(project_id, title, assignee_id, deadline, description)

    def get_task(self, task_id) -> Task:
        return self.tasks.get(task_id)

    def list_tasks_by_project(self, project_id) -> List[Task]:
        return self.tasks.list_by_project(project_id)

    def list_tasks_by_assignee(self, assignee_id) -> List[Task]:
        return self.tasks.list_by_assignee(assignee_id)

    def assign_task(self, task_id, assignee_id) -> Task:
        return self.tasks.assign(task_id, assignee_id)

    def reassign_task(self, task_id, assignee_id) -> Task:
        return self.tasks.reassign(task_id, assignee_id)

    def unassign_task(self, task_id) -> Task:
        return self.tasks.unassign(task_id)

    def set_deadline(self, task_id, deadline) -> Task:
        return self.tasks.set_deadline(task_id, deadline)

    def update_status(self, task_id, status) -> Task:
        return self.tasks.update_status(task_id, status)

    # -- messages -----------------------------------------------------------
    def post_message(
        self,
        author_id,
        body,
        project_id=None,
        task_id=None,
        parent_id=None,
        attachments=None,
    ) -> Message:
        return self.messages.post(
            author_id,
            body,
            project_id=project_id,
            task_id=task_id,
            parent_id=parent_id,
            attachments=attachments,
        )

    def edit_message(self, message_id, editor_id, new_body) -> Message:
        return self.messages.edit(message_id, editor_id, new_body)

    def add_attachment(self, message_id, user_id, filename, data) -> Message:
        return self.messages.add_attachment(message_id, user_id, filename, data)

    def list_messages_by_project(self, project_id) -> List[Message]:
        return self.messages.list_by_project(project_id)

    def list_messages_by_task(self, task_id) -> List[Message]:
        return self.messages.list_by_task(task_id)

    def delete_message(self, message_id, user_id) -> None:
        return self.messages.delete(message_id, user_id)

    # -- performance --------------------------------------------------------
    def get_completion_rate(self, user_id) -> float:
        return self.performance.completion_rate(user_id)

    def get_average_completion_time(self, user_id) -> Optional[float]:
        return self.performance.average_completion_time(user_id)

    def submit_rating(
        self, rater_id, ratee_id, score, feedback="", task_id=None
    ) -> Rating:
        return self.performance.submit_rating(
            rater_id, ratee_id, score, feedback, task_id
        )

    def get_ratings_summary(self, user_id) -> Dict[str, object]:
        return self.performance.ratings_summary(user_id)

    def get_user_dashboard(self, user_id) -> Dict[str, object]:
        return self.performance.user_dashboard(user_id)

    # -- reports ------------------------------------------------------------
    def project_progress_report(self, project_id) -> Dict[str, object]:
        return self.reports.project_progress(project_id)

    def team_performance_report(self, project_id=None) -> Dict[str, object]:
        return self.reports.team_performance(project_id)

    def individual_performance_report(self, user_id) -> Dict[str, object]:
        return self.reports.individual_performance(user_id)

    def export_report(self, report, report_format):
        return self.reports.export(report, report_format)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Demonstration (only runs when executed directly)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def _demo() -> None:  # pragma: no cover - illustrative only
    from datetime import timedelta

    manager = TeamCollaborationManager()
    owner = manager.register_user("ada", "ada@example.com", UserRole.ADMIN)
    worker = manager.register_user("grace", "grace@example.com")

    start = datetime.now()
    project = manager.create_project(
        "Apollo", start, start + timedelta(days=30), owner.id, "Moon shot"
    )
    task = manager.create_task(
        project.id, "Design rocket", assignee_id=worker.id,
        deadline=start + timedelta(days=10),
    )
    manager.update_status(task.id, TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS)
    manager.update_status(task.id, TaskStatus.COMPLETED)

    manager.post_message(owner.id, "Great work!", task_id=task.id)
    manager.submit_rating(owner.id, worker.id, 5, "Outstanding")

    print("Dashboard:", manager.get_user_dashboard(worker.id))
    report = manager.team_performance_report()
    print("Team report rows:", report["rows"])
    print("CSV:\n" + manager.export_report(report, ReportFormat.CSV))
    pdf = manager.export_report(
        manager.project_progress_report(project.id), ReportFormat.PDF
    )
    print("PDF bytes start with %PDF-:", pdf[:5] == b"%PDF-")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    _demo()
